groups.label: Orchestration
groups.mission: Routes every request, owns scope, owns technical standards. The two interface layers of MISHKAN — PM (Nehemiah) talks to Y4NN, CTO (Bezalel) gates technical quality.
groups.charter: No specialist work happens without scope (Nehemiah) and technical fit (Bezalel) approved first.

agents.description: Scope, delivery, sprint state, user interface in exploration mode.
Technical standards, architecture, quality bar.

groups.label: Research Pipeline
groups.mission: Shared research pipeline reachable by any agent facing an unknown. Six stages: clarify → formulate → research → summarise → evaluate → report.
groups.charter: No fabricated facts. If it's unknown, the pipeline runs. The report carries verifiable sources or marks the unknown explicitly.

agents.description: Establishes the threshold — clarifies what the question really is before research starts.
Structures the research plan with precision.
Goes wide and returns a faithful, fearless report.
Compresses Caleb's findings into actionable summary.
Discerns true signal from false in the research output.
Carries the final message back faithfully.

groups.label: Chosheb
groups.hebrew_meaning: Cunning work — deviser of artistic works (Exodus 31:4)
groups.mission: Designs the visible and felt experience before a line of frontend code is written. Owns UI, UX, design system tokens, accessibility intent.
groups.charter: Design handoff to Panim is complete or it doesn't ship. DS fit, a11y, asset manifest, QA matrix — all four lenses or the package is incomplete.

agents.description: Bezalel's partner — leads design craftsmen, coordinates handoff.
Crafts the visible beautiful things — UI design and prototypes.
Sees what others miss — cognitive and emotional UX guidance.
Carries the team's structured output faithfully.

groups.label: Panim
groups.hebrew_meaning: Face · presence · what is turned toward (Exodus 33:14)
groups.mission: Implements every visible surface — components, pages, design system code, asset pipeline, a11y/SEO. Consumes Chosheb's handoff, contracts with Yasad's APIs.
groups.charter: What the user sees is correct, accessible, and consistent with the DS. Token changes propagate everywhere or nowhere — no half-rolled-out themes.
groups.relationships: Chosheb → Panim (design handoff) · Panim ↔ Yasad (API contracts)

agents.description: Leads all visible frontend works — cunning across stack.
Keeper of patterns and standards across components.
Implements visible form — the builder of frontend code.
Faithful servant who supplies and sustains assets.
Makes the work heard and received by all — SEO + a11y.
Sees what others miss — frontend QA, evaluates without producing.
Carries the team's structured output faithfully.

groups.label: Yasad
groups.hebrew_meaning: To lay the foundation · establish solidly (Psalm 24:2)
groups.mission: Lays and maintains the backend foundation — endpoints, contracts, data schema, persistence, business invariants. Contract-first.
groups.charter: Contracts before code. Migrations reviewed by 4 orthogonal lenses (contracts/perf/security/tests) before they ship.
groups.relationships: Panim ↔ Yasad (API contracts) · Yasad ↔ Mishmar (audit + remediation)

agents.description: Governor who lays the deep base — leads backend foundation work.
Keeper of what must not change — backend patterns.
Brings architectural vision — what should and should not be built.
Strength of Yah — does the direct backend implementation labour.
Keeper of what is stored — database design and migration.
Absolute integrity — holds the line on backend standards.
Reports faithfully from his section.

groups.label: Mishmar
groups.hebrew_meaning: Guard · watch · the post set day and night (Nehemiah 4:9)
groups.mission: Cross-cutting security — code, surface, infrastructure, threat model. A constraint shaping every team's output from the start, not a team reviewing at the end.
groups.charter: Sensitive surface changes pass the 3-lens gate (code-OWASP / surface / threat-model) with adversarial refute before merge. Hardening overlay re-applied on every container recreate.
groups.relationships: Mishmar ↔ all teams (cross-cutting audit) · Mishmar → Migdal (security gates infra)

agents.description: Acted decisively to stop a breach — zealous for the boundary.
Mighty man who handles the hardest infrastructure threats.
Field general across all attack vectors — app surface security.
Keeps watch at the code level — security ops.
Trusted advisor who outmanoeuvred the attacker — security counsel.
Carries the structured account faithfully.

groups.label: Migdal
groups.hebrew_meaning: Tower · load-bearing structures of the wall (Nehemiah 3:1)
groups.mission: Builds and operates the load-bearing infrastructure — systems, network, devops pipelines, observability, recovery. Gated by Mishmar at change time.
groups.charter: Infra change passes the 5-lens panel (design/systems/devops/observability/health) before apply. No `:latest` tags. Hardening overlay always re-applied. DR drilled quarterly.
groups.relationships: Mishmar → Migdal (security gates) · Migdal serves all teams' runtime

agents.description: Organises the foundational infrastructure work.
Works at the structural intersection — OS / virt / networks.
Designs connections between infrastructure parts.
Long support stretch — devsecops practice, operational support.
Works at the delivery layer — pipelines, deploys.
Watches for risk and advises — infrastructure health.
Keeps record, mindful of what happened.

groups.label: Sefer
groups.hebrew_meaning: Book · scroll · official written record (Nehemiah 8, Ezra 6)
groups.mission: Documentation across three layers — organisation (Seraiah), project (Joah), team (Shevna). Pull-based: pulls at milestones and on trigger events, not on every commit.
groups.charter: What is not written down is forgotten. Release notes assembled from git log per category. ADRs for every consequential decision. Style guide applied uniformly.
groups.relationships: Sefer ← all teams (pulls at milestones + trigger events)

agents.description: First Recorder in David's court — cares for national archives.
Operates at the highest state level — org-wide documentation.
Documents specific events and decisions of each project.
Embedded with teams — documents their specific outputs.
Takes knowledge and makes it legible for others.
Verifies, interprets, reports back with authority.
